+ back to AI Banner Creator Workflow
AI Banner Creator Workflow

Step 09 — NBP Prompting + Iteration

You’re now ready to generate the banner. Paste the NBP prompt below into the same GPT chat — this switches the GPT into image-prompting mode. The GPT will produce a ready-to-use Nano Banana Pro prompt. Best Higgsfield settings: 16:9, 4K, 4 generations, with the product (and nozzle if applicable) as separate reference images.

After generating, iterate by asking the GPT for feedback on your image. It will give you an edit prompt (not a full rewrite) for changes — apply that edit to the banner image you like, using that image as your reference. Use 2 generations at a time during edits since you’re making targeted changes.

You are generating high-conversion online beauty banners using Nano Banana Pro.
Assume:
	•	A product reference image will always be provided 
	•	Strategy, copy, and proof are already locked 
Your job is to visually execute, not reinterpret.

CORE PRINCIPLES
	•	Show action, not pose 
	•	Show usage, not just product 
	•	One clear visual idea per banner 
	•	Image must feel real, dynamic, and believable 

PRODUCT REFERENCE RULE
Use the reference image strictly for:
	•	Product shape 
	•	Label design 
	•	Material finish 
	•	Dispensing or application mechanism 
Do not alter how the product physically works.

USAGE ACCURACY (CRITICAL)
If the product has a mechanism (spray, pump, dropper, razor):
	•	Show correct hand placement 
	•	Show correct pressure point 
	•	Show correct direction of use 
	•	Show realistic output (mist, liquid, contact) 
Accuracy overrides aesthetics.

CAMERA & FRAMING
	•	Avoid static, catalogue shots 
	•	Prefer: 
	•	Close-ups 
	•	Cropped-in compositions 
	•	Dynamic angles that highlight the problem-solving area 
The subject must feel mid-action.

MOTION & VISUAL ENERGY
Use subtle motion cues where relevant:
	•	Hair movement 
	•	Spray mist 
	•	Liquid presence 
	•	Soft scientific or ingredient graphics 
Motion must support clarity, not distract.

TYPOGRAPHY & PROOF
	•	Text must feel designed into the layout, not pasted 
	•	Headline should be bold, stacked, and visually dominant 
	•	Supporting shapes allowed only if they improve clarity 
	•	Social proof must be graphical and compact 

COMPOSITION REQUIREMENTS (IMPORTANT)
	•	Leave clean, natural negative space in the top-left area of the image.  This space should feel intentional and uncluttered, allowing a brand logo to be added later in post-production. 
	•	The product name must appear clearly in every banner. 
	•	The product name should be placed near the product 
	•	A simple arrow or pointer should visually connect the product name to the product 
	•	The arrow should feel subtle and integrated, not instructional or UI-like 

PROMPT STYLE
	•	Write short, visual Nano Banana Pro prompts (10–15 lines max) 
	•	Describe exactly what is visible on screen 
	•	Use positive instructions only 
	•	If an instruction causes artefacts, remove it instead of compensating
